alignments.preprint_claim_text: The additive margin Softmax (AM-Softmax) objective function is proposed for deep face verification. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed additive margin softmax learning objective function improves face verification performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication moves from proposing the objective to explicitly claiming empirical improvement in face verification, clarifying the contribution and strengthening its practical impact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AM-Softmax introduces a novel additive angular margin for the Softmax loss that is more interpretable than existing methods. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Introducing a large-margin strategy into the classification model encourages intraclass variance minimization. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the large-margin effect on intraclass variance rather than the interpretability claim; the focus and certainty are changed, so the original interpretability claim is revised and narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments show that the additive margin softmax loss consistently performs better than current state-of-the-art methods using the same network architecture and training dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments on LFW and MegaFace demonstrate that the proposed algorithm outperforms existing methods at very low false alarm rates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents more specific experimental settings and a targeted performance claim (datasets and low false alarm regimes), narrowing and clarifying the preprint's broader performance statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A TIR pedestrian tracking dataset has been developed for the evaluation of TIR pedestrian trackers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We develop a TIR pedestrian tracking dataset for the TIR pedestrian tracker evaluation.
alignments.rationale: Wording and meaning are effectively identical; both state the creation of the dataset for evaluation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset includes 60 thermal sequences with manual annotations and nine attribute labels for evaluation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset includes 60 thermal sequences with manual annotations and nine attribute labels for evaluation.
alignments.rationale: Claim text is the same in both versions, identical scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Large-scale evaluation experiments on the benchmark dataset reveal the strengths and weaknesses of nine publicly available trackers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental results help us understand the strengths and weaknesses of nine publicly available trackers.
alignments.rationale: Publication rephrases the preprint claim with the same content but with slightly lower reported certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The functions of the TIR pedestrian tracker can be divided into three components: feature extractor, motion model, and observation model.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly state the categorical decomposition into three components; related experimental claims exist but the explicit structural claim is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Comparison experiments validate how each component affects the tracker's performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We conduct three comparison experiments to validate how each component affects the tracker's performance.
alignments.rationale: The core claim is the same, but the publication frames it more definitively (higher certainty) and specifies three experiments.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed systematic sampling and cyclic/sequential sampling techniques can reduce data access time in machine learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed systematic and cyclic sampling techniques can reduce training time by decreasing data access time.
alignments.rationale: Publication clarifies the practical effect (reduced training time) while retaining the original mechanism (reduced data access time).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The theoretical results demonstrate that systematic sampling, cyclic sampling, and random sampling have the same convergence in expectation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Theoretical results demonstrate that systematic and cyclic sampling achieve similar convergence to random sampling in expectation.
alignments.rationale: The theoretical equivalence in expectation is stated the same way in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results show that the proposed sampling techniques can achieve up to six times faster training compared to traditional methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results indicate that the proposed sampling techniques can lead to up to six times faster training on benchmarked datasets.
alignments.rationale: The numeric experimental claim is preserved but the publication tones down certainty and specifies benchmarked datasets.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Stochastic domination should be used more frequently in the mathematical runtime analysis of evolutionary algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Stochastic domination should be used more frequently in the mathematical runtime analysis of evolutionary algorithms.
alignments.rationale: Conclusion is identically stated in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We prove a fitness level theorem which shows that the runtime is dominated by a sum of independent geometric random variables.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We prove a variant of the fitness level theorem which shows that the runtime of the search heuristic is dominated by a sum of independent geometric random variables.
alignments.rationale: Publication specifies this is a variant of the fitness-level theorem and phrases the subject as a search heuristic, a slight technical clarification of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We prove the first tail bounds for several classic runtime problems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We prove the first tail bounds for several classic runtime problems.
alignments.rationale: Claim is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The runtime of any $(,p)$ mutation-based algorithm on any function with unique optimum is subdominated by the runtime of a variant of the  on the  function.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The runtime of any (μ, p) mutation-based algorithm on any function with unique optimum is subdominated by the runtime of a variant of the (1 + 1) EA on the OneMax function.
alignments.rationale: Notation is clarified in the publication but the mathematical content of the dominance/subdomination claim is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We determine the fastest unbiased (1+1) algorithm for the adingones benchmark problem.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We determine the fastest unbiased (1 + 1) algorithm for the LeadingOnes benchmark problem.
alignments.rationale: The claim is the same; the publication corrects notation/typo and makes the benchmark name explicit.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed implementation of the Nerlove--Arrow model effectively explains the relationship between advertising expenditures and weekly sales for a fast-food franchise network.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed implementation of the Nerlove‐Arrow model effectively explains the relationship between advertising expenditures and weekly sales for a fast-food franchise network.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim and scope in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The flexibility and modularity of the Bayesian structural time series model allow for generalization to other markets or situations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The flexibility and modularity of the Bayesian structural time series model allow for generalization to other markets or situations.
alignments.rationale: Same wording and meaning across versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Bayesian nature of the model enables the incorporation of  a priori information, which can be updated with relevant data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Bayesian nature of the model enables the incorporation of a priori information that can be updated with relevant data.
alignments.rationale: Claim is the same in both preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model will be used to present a strategy of budget scheduling across time and channels.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model supports managerial decision-making regarding budget scheduling of advertising across time and channels.
alignments.rationale: Publication reframes the future-use phrasing into a concrete managerial support statement, clarifying practical application.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Computer-aided diagnosis (CAD) tools can improve the accuracy and speed of tumor risk stratification from radiology images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Computer-aided diagnosis (CAD) tools can improve the accuracy and speed of tumor risk stratification from radiology images.
alignments.rationale: Identical conclusion in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Significant gains in tumor characterization can be achieved using deep learning algorithms, particularly with a 3D Convolutional Neural Network and Transfer Learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Significant gains in tumor characterization can be achieved using deep learning algorithms, particularly with a 3D convolutional neural network and transfer learning.
alignments.rationale: Same substantive claim with only minor formatting differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating task dependent feature representations into a CAD system via a graph-regularized sparse Multi-Task Learning (MTL) framework enhances tumor characterization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating task-dependent feature representations into a CAD system via a graph-regularized sparse multi-task learning framework enhances tumor characterization.
alignments.rationale: Claim content is preserved exactly, aside from minor stylistic edits.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed proportion-SVM algorithm addresses the challenge of limited labeled training data in unsupervised tumor classification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed proportion-support vector machine can effectively characterize tumors in the context of limited labeled training data.
alignments.rationale: Publication restates the contribution as an effective characterization result in the limited-label setting, clarifying practical performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed supervised and unsupervised learning algorithms achieve state-of-the-art sensitivity and specificity results in lung and pancreas tumor diagnosis challenges.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed supervised and unsupervised learning algorithms achieve state-of-the-art sensitivity and specificity results in lung and pancreas tumor diagnosis challenges.
alignments.rationale: Identical performance claim in both preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proper utilisation of concentration of measure phenomena in machine learning can transform the curse of dimensionality into a blessing. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proper utilization of measure concentration phenomena in machine learning can transform the curse of dimensionality into a blessing of dimensionality. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Wording is effectively identical and the conclusion and certainty are preserved; only a stylistic addition "of dimensionality" was made.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recently discovered phenomena of measure concentration simplify some machine learning problems in high dimensions and allow for the correction of legacy artificial intelligence systems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recently discovered phenomena of measure concentration drastically simplify some machine learning problems in high dimensions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the simplification claim but omits the specific claim about correcting legacy AI systems and uses stronger adverbial wording, narrowing the scope of the original contribution and removing the correction application.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new stochastic separation theorems indicate that random points are linearly separable from the rest of the set, even for exponentially large random sets. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new stochastic separation theorems indicate that random points are linearly separable from the rest of the set, even for exponentially large random sets. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The statement is replicated verbatim with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The stochastic separation theorems provide classifiers and a non-iterative procedure for learning that can separate erroneous samples from correct ones. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The stochastic separation theorems provide classifiers that enable non-destructive correction of errors in artificial intelligence systems. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Core idea that the theorems yield classifiers addressing errors is preserved, but the publication reframes the outcome as enabling non-destructive correction rather than emphasizing a non-iterative separation procedure, a clarification of emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm EBIC is probably the first one capable of discovering with accuracy exceeding 50% multiple complex patterns in real gene expression datasets. (certainty=low, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel biclustering algorithm EBIC is capable of discovering multiple complex patterns with unprecedented accuracy in real gene expression datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EBIC outperforms state-of-the-art biclustering methods, in terms of recovery and relevance, on both synthetic and genetic datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: EBIC greatly outperforms state-of-the-art biclustering methods, in terms of recovery and relevance, on both synthetic and genetic datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EBIC yields results over 12 times faster than the most accurate reference algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: EBIC yields results over 12 times faster than the most accurate reference algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: A set of axioms required for any reasonable inconsistency index is not exhaustive and can be expanded by adding two new properties. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The results regarding triads can contribute to the measurement of inconsistency for pairwise comparison matrices with more than three alternatives. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The 3D Densely Connected Super-Resolution Networks (DCSRN) outperforms bicubic interpolation in restoring 4x resolution-reduced images. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DCSRN also surpasses other deep learning methods in restoring high-resolution features from low-resolution structural brain MR images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DCSRN also surpasses other deep learning methods in restoring high-resolution features from low-resolution structural brain MR images. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Single Image Super-Resolution (SISR) techniques have improved dramatically due to recent breakthroughs in deep learning. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Evorus can automate itself without compromising conversation quality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Evorus can automate itself without compromising conversation quality. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The architecture of Evorus allows future researchers to make further innovation on the underlying automated components. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TextBoxes++ detects arbitrary-oriented scene text with both high accuracy and efficiency in a single network forward pass. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TextBoxes++ detects arbitrary-oriented scene text with both high accuracy and efficiency in a single network forward pass. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: TextBoxes++ outperforms competing methods in terms of text localization accuracy and runtime. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TextBoxes++ achieves an f-measure of 0.817 at 11.6fps for 1024*1024 ICDAR 2015 Incidental text images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TextBoxes++ achieves an f-measure of 0.817 at 11.6fps for 1024 × 1024 ICDAR 2015 Incidental text images. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TextBoxes++ significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art approaches for word spotting and end-to-end text recognition tasks on popular benchmarks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TextBoxes++ significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art approaches for word spotting and end-to-end text recognition tasks on popular benchmarks. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A dendritic-inspired processing architecture and novel CMOS neuron circuits can transform the stochastic behavior of binary NVMs into a useful leverage for biologically-plausible STDP learning. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The probability that an edit is accepted is a function of the editor's skill, the difficulty of editing the component, and a user-component interaction term. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
